title: 2-Step Security
body_text:
2-Step Security
1	Introduction to 2-Step Security
1.1	Overview of 2-Step Security
2-step security provides an extra layer of authentication when accessing certain applications (e.g., User Management), or functionality within an application (e.g., approving outgoing wires in the Wires application). When prompted, the user must enter a randomly generated one-time password from a registered 2-step security token. The user must have physical access to the token to retrieve a one-time password. A User Management Security Officer (Security Officer) assigns a 2-step security token to Client Centre Users, Business Users, and other Security Officers via User Management. See the User Management Part for procedures.
There are two types of security tokens: soft tokens and hard tokens. A soft token is a security token that芒聙聶s added to an authentication app on a user芒聙聶s mobile device (e.g., Google Authenticator). Soft token apps generate a one-time password every 30 seconds. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Soft Token
A hard token is a physical device that generates a random one-time password when activated. Hard token passwords refresh every 60 seconds. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Hard Token
